title: LongMemEval temporal retrieval uses question-date metadata
summary: Kage recall now expands relative temporal phrases when the query includes explicit date metadata such as 'Question date: 2023/03/28'. The LongMemEval S benchmark harness uses this retrieval time question date for relativ
body:
Kage recall now expands relative temporal phrases when the query includes explicit date metadata such as 'Question date: 2023/03/28'. The LongMemEval-S benchmark harness uses this retrieval-time question date for relative temporal questions. Full LongMemEval-S retrieval now scores R@5 96.17%, R@10 98.72%, R@20 99.79%, MRR 0.9099, NDCG@10 0.9283 over 470 non-abstention questions. This is evidence retrieval, not answer accuracy.
Evidence: Implemented in mcp/kernel.ts, covered in mcp/kernel.test.ts, benchmark harnesses updated in benchmarks/longmemeval-kage-retrieval.mjs and benchmarks/longmemeval-bm25-baseline.mjs, and documented in README.md and benchmarks/LONGMEMEVAL.md.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp; node benchmarks/longmemeval-kage-retrieval.mjs --data /private/tmp/kage-external-bench/longmemeval_s_cleaned.json --limit 470 --top-k 10 --out /private/tmp/kage-external-bench/longmemeval-kage-full-temporal.json
